etymology.description: semantic ⺼(肉) (ròu, "meat; flesh") + phonetic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") -> stomach; belly
etymology.interpretation: The character 肚(dù, "stomach; belly") combines the semantic component ⺼(肉) (ròu, "meat; flesh") indicating the physical body or flesh, with the phonetic component 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") which provides the sound. This formation emphasizes the body part 'stomach' or 'belly', drawing on the notion of the fleshy part of the body.

examples.chinese: 他的肚子(他的肚子)很饿(很餓)。
她感到肚子疼痛(她感到肚子疼痛)，于是去看医生(於是去看醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào dùzi téngtòng, yúshì qù kàn yīshēng.
examples.english: She felt stomach pains, so she went to see the doctor.

examples.chinese: 小猫睡在我的肚子上(小貓睡在我的肚子上)很可爱(很可愛)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo māo shuì zài wǒ de dùzi shàng hěn kě'ài.
examples.english: The kitten sleeping on my belly is very cute.
